for addr in 0000000000222098 0000000000222234 000000000019bac0 000000000019bc14; do
  dec_addr=$(printf "%d" "0x$addr")
  match=$(awk -v addr="$dec_addr" '{
    sym_start = strtonum("0x"$1)
    sym_size = strtonum($2)
    if (addr >= sym_start && addr < sym_start + sym_size) {
      printf "%s+0x%x", $3, addr - sym_start
    }
  }' mono_syms_sized.txt)
  if [ -z "$match" ]; then
    # Fallback: nearest symbol before address
    match=$(awk -v addr="$dec_addr" '{
      sym_start = strtonum("0x"$1)
      if (sym_start <= addr) { last_name=$3; last_start=sym_start }
    } END { printf "%s+0x%x", last_name, addr - last_start }' mono_syms_sized.txt)
    echo "pc $addr -> $match (nearest, no size match)"
  else
    echo "pc $addr -> $match"
  fi
done

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-d72f55466eba4255a624423fe35b549a/symbols

# Use aarch64 readelf to get all dynamic symbols with sizes, sorted
aarch64-linux-gnu-readelf -sW libmonosgen-2.0.so | awk '$4 == "FUNC" && $7 != "UND" && strtonum("0x"$2) > 0 { printf "0x%s %d %s\n", $2, strtonum($3), $8 }' | sort > mono_funcs.txt
wc -l mono_funcs.txt

# For each PC, find the symbol whose range contains it
python3 << 'EOF'
import sys

# Load symbols
syms = []
with open("mono_funcs.txt") as f:
    for line in f:
        parts = line.strip().split()
        addr = int(parts[0], 16)
        size = int(parts[1])
        name = parts[2] if len(parts) > 2 else "?"
        syms.append((addr, size, name))

syms.sort()

pcs = [0x222098, 0x222234, 0x19bac0, 0x19bc14]
frames = {0x222098: "#01", 0x222234: "#02", 0x19bac0: "#03", 0x19bc14: "#06"}

for pc in pcs:
    # Try exact range match first
    found = False
    for addr, size, name in syms:
        if size > 0 and addr <= pc < addr + size:
            print(f"  {frames[pc]} pc {pc:#010x}  {name}+{pc-addr:#x}")
            found = True
            break
    if not found:
        # Nearest below
        best = None
        for addr, size, name in syms:
            if addr <= pc:
                best = (addr, size, name)
        if best:
            addr, size, name = best
            print(f"  {frames[pc]} pc {pc:#010x}  {name}+{pc-addr:#x} (nearest)")
        else:
            print(f"  {frames[pc]} pc {pc:#010x}  ???")
EOF

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-d72f55466eba4255a624423fe35b549a/symbols

# Let's symbolicate with what we have. Use the .dbg file for libmonosgen
file symdir/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg
aarch64-linux-gnu-readelf -sW symdir/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg | wc -l

# Resolve all 4 mono addresses using the debug symbols
python3 << 'EOF'
import subprocess, re

# Get all symbols from debug file
result = subprocess.run(
    ["aarch64-linux-gnu-readelf", "-sW", "symdir/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg"],
    capture_output=True, text=True
)

syms = []
for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
    parts = line.split()
    if len(parts) >= 8 and parts[3] == "FUNC":
        try:
            addr = int(parts[1], 16)
            size = int(parts[2])
            name = parts[7]
            if addr > 0:
                syms.append((addr, size, name))
        except ValueError:
            continue

syms.sort()
print(f"Loaded {len(syms)} function symbols")

# Frames from tombstone for libmonosgen-2.0.so
frames = [
    ("#01", 0x222098),
    ("#02", 0x222234),
    ("#03", 0x19bac0),
    ("#06", 0x19bc14),
]

for fid, pc in frames:
    found = False
    for addr, size, name in syms:
        if size > 0 and addr <= pc < addr + size:
            print(f"  {fid} pc {pc:#010x}  {name}+{pc-addr:#x}")
            found = True
            break
    if not found:
        # Nearest below
        best = None
        for addr, size, name in syms:
            if addr <= pc:
                best = (addr, size, name)
        if best:
            a, s, n = best
            print(f"  {fid} pc {pc:#010x}  {n}+{pc-a:#x} (nearest, no size)")
        else:
            print(f"  {fid} pc {pc:#010x}  ???")
EOF
